mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 0.3% monthly increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days as investors price in recession risks.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite negative signal, or UNRATE increases fail to correlate with SPX returns (Pearson r < 0.1).

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in real consumer spending (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) indicates strong aggregate demand, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days through improved corporate earnings expectations.
failure_condition: Rank IC drops below 0.05 for any 3-year window, or 60-day returns in high-demand regimes are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 5% monthly decline in industrial production (INDPRO) signals weakening supply-side capacity, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days due to reduced corporate profitability.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite negative signal, or INDPRO declines fail to correlate with SPX returns (Pearson r < 0.1).

mechanism_narrative: A 0.5% monthly increase in CPI inflation (CPIAUCSL) combined with stable federal funds rates (FEDFUNDS) indicates persistent inflationary pressures, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days through central bank tightening expectations.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL rises > 0.5pp in 1 month and FEDFUNDS < 0.1pp change
failure_condition: Rank IC drops below 0.05 for any 3-year window, or 20-day returns in high-inflation regimes are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 100,000+ monthly increase in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) signals a tightening labor market, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days through improved consumer spending and corporate margins.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite positive signal, or PAYEMS increases fail to correlate with SPX returns (Pearson r < 0.1).

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly GDP growth (GDPC1) above 3% indicates strong economic momentum, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days through sustained corporate earnings growth.
failure_condition: Rank IC drops below 0.05 for any 3-year window, or 60-day returns in high-GDP growth regimes are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 2% monthly decline in industrial production (INDPRO) combined with rising commodity prices (CPIAUCSL) indicates supply-side bottlenecks, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days through stagflation risks.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO falls > 2% in 1 month and CPIAUCSL rises > 0.3pp in 1 month
failure_condition: Rank IC drops below 0.05 for any 3-year window, or 20-day returns in stagflation regimes are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).
